summary: German-born American artist known for sculptures exploring the body, bodily fluids, and feminist themes. Famous for beeswax and bronze figures, wolf-and-woman motifs, and works addressing the AIDS crisis. Daughter of minimalist sculptor Tony Smith.

works.name: Rapture (2001)
works.description: Bronze sculpture depicting a nude woman stepping out from the stomach of a wolf. One of Smith's signature wolf-and-woman motifs. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Nude woman stepping out from a wolf
examples: An artist with this first name showed a nude woman stepping out from a wolf in the sculpture Rapture, one of her many artworks using woman and wolf motifs.
A German artist with this first name depicted a woman stepping out of the stomach of a wolf in her sculpture Rapture.

works.name: Mary Magdalene (1994)
works.description: Silicon bronze sculpture of Mary Magdalene with flayed skin, dragging a chain. Often compared to Donatello's wooden Penitent Magdalene.

clues.clue: Silicon bronze sculpture of Mary Magdalene with flayed skin and dragging a chain
examples: Kiki Smith created a silicon bronze sculpture of this woman with flayed skin and dragging a chain.

works.name: Body Fluid Works (water-cooler bottles, beeswax figures)
works.description: Series of works exploring bodily fluids and the body during the AIDS crisis. Includes twelve glass water-cooler bottles labeled with fluids like 'blood' and 'pus,' and life-sized beeswax sculptures of naked humans excreting body fluids.

clues.clue: Row of twelve glass water-cooler bottles labeled 'blood,' 'pus,' etc.
examples: An artist with this surname created a row of twelve glass water-cooler bottles with labels such as "blood" and "pus."

clues.clue: Life-sized beeswax sculptures of naked humans excreting body fluids
examples: has used beeswax to create many life-sized sculptures of naked humans excreting various body fluids.

clues.clue: Painted internal organs and put bodily fluids in water jugs during the AIDS crisis
examples: A wax sculptor with this first name, who painted internal organs and put bodily fluids in water jugs during the AIDS crisis, has the last name Smith.

works.name: Ink Drawing of Woman with Wolf
works.description: An ink work depicting a nude woman lying with a wolf, part of Smith's recurring wolf motif.

comprehensive_summary: Kiki Smith is a German-born American sculptor and printmaker whose work centers on the human body, bodily fluids, and feminist themes. In quiz bowl, she appears most often through her sculpture Rapture (2001), which depicts a nude woman stepping out from the stomach of a wolf — a signature motif of wolves and women that recurs throughout her oeuvre. Her AIDS-crisis-era works are also frequently clued: a row of twelve glass water-cooler bottles labeled with bodily fluids like 'blood' and 'pus,' as well as life-sized beeswax sculptures of naked humans excreting body fluids. She painted internal organs and put bodily fluids in water jugs during this period. Her silicon bronze Mary Magdalene, shown with flayed skin dragging a chain, is compared alongside Donatello's wooden Penitent Magdalene. Smith is often identified as German and as a wax sculptor, and her surname connects her to Tony Smith (minimalist sculptor of Die) and David Smith (creator of the Cubi series). She also created an ink work of a nude woman lying with a wolf. Power marks tend to be her specific works (Rapture, water-cooler bottles), while giveaways reference the Smith surname shared with Tony and David.
